chapters.title: Don’t rush into marriage until you’re certain you’re making the right choice for your future
chapters.text:
Marriage is like a double-edged sword; it can be sweet, and it can be bitter, depending on what the persons involved make it to be. A successful marriage is a great source of joy and untold happiness. But a frustrating marriage, on the other hand, is the closest experience any two people can have to the horror of hell. Yes, it’s that serious. And that’s why you need to be extra careful when choosing a life partner.

Far too many singles go into marriage blank. Many people don’t think about what they want their marriage to look like in the next 10, 20, 40, or 50 years. We have long-term goals for our careers, finances, and every other thing we deem important. But when it comes to marriage, the average single person just knows that they want a “happy home.” Ask them “How?” and they won’t have much to say. That’s terrible, but it’s about to change for you with this summary.

Admittedly, the “in-love” feeling gets us on an emotional high. Some people get off this rollercoaster earlier than others, but research has shown that by the 24-month mark, you’d have come to the point where you aren’t entirely under the influence of your emotions. It’s at this point that you begin to see the seemingly hidden flaws in your partner, as well as other elements of your relationship that you easily overlooked in the first 24 months.

Every infatuated person believes their partner is the best human being in the world until they get off the emotional cliff.

It's important to take things slow and wait for the reactions in your brain to calm down before making a lifelong commitment. But it's even more effective if you can learn from an experienced counselor. That way, you save yourself a lot of time and emotional trauma. That's where this summary comes in. What you're about to read will:
• Equip you with the wisdom you need to decide what exactly you should want in a lifelong relationship.
• Show you traits to imbibe for a successful marriage.
• Reveal why soulmate is a myth, and show you why there's not just one person you can marry.

chapters.title: Stop looking out for the less important things
chapters.text:
Marriage is a whole package; many things go into it, and all the things you may be looking at are quite important. There’s just a priority to it.

Although this isn’t one of those religious teachings that tell you to completely forget about sexual appeal, it’s important to marry someone you’re sexually attracted to. But don’t settle for a man or woman that you can’t imagine seeing their nakedness — sexual attraction mustn’t be number one on your priority list.

The reason is simple: sexual chemistry may sustain a relationship, but it’s not enough to take you through the lifelong commitment that marriage requires.

Many women are led into marriage primarily through romantic idealism, and many men are swept to the altar through sexual attraction. Before you can make a wise marital choice, you have to rid yourself of inferior motivations. The wrong why will lead you to the wrong who. ~ Gary L. Thomas

Generally, many people prioritize romance and sexual attraction above character when they’re dating. But a few years into the marriage, they realize that the most important trait in their partner isn’t their ability to make their hearts race or get their sexual drives heated up, but the quality of their character.

You don’t want to repeat the mistakes of other couples; that’s why you picked this summary. So here’s something for you to do:

Try to scrutinize your partner’s character. Think very deeply because emotions can be deceiving.
• Ask yourself, would you want this person to become the father or mother of your kids?
• Judging by their character and lifestyle choices, what would your marriage look like 10 years from now?
• When the kids are running around, the bills are piling up, and you’re faced with challenging life situations, would they make good companions, or would they flee from you?

A mere sexual partner is almost always a fair-weather friend, but a true lover would go through thick and thin with you, who loves you deeply, and is committed to meeting your needs in unselfish ways unconditionally.

Marry someone who loves you, not someone who is only interested in you sexually.

Bonus point: you’re more likely to enjoy lasting sexual satisfaction from a selfless person because sex in itself is a selfless act.

Did you know? You attract the kind of person that you are. If you’re out to play with people’s emotions, you’re more likely to attract people who will play with yours. And if you’re a selfless lover, you’re likely to settle down with someone just like you, so work on yourself.

chapters.title: The concept of a soul mate is a myth
chapters.text:
The idea of having only one soul mate who completes us was made popular by Plato, one of the greatest Greek philosophers to walk the planet.

According to Plato, there was a time when a group of superhumans surfaced upon the earth. These people were two (male and female) in one, which made them so powerful they threatened to destroy the gods.

The only way to stop them was for Zeus to split each “whole” into male and female halves. According to the legend, this made the superhumans so weak and vulnerable they could no longer fight the gods and spent the rest of their lives trying to reunite with their soul mates — the soul they were connected to before. Plato went on to suggest that this legend is the reason human beings desire to be joined to one another.

As a Christian, you must understand that this isn’t true. It’s just a myth. The Bible clearly states that God, Himself, created male and female humans and instituted marriage. However, the Bible does not say you can’t be happy as a single man or woman, nor that there’s only one human created for you to marry.

The reason for marriage is that two are better than one. That’s it, no more, no less. And marriage should be something that helps you pursue God’s kingdom, not what pushes you from it.

Being single is not a crime. It’s better to be single and pursue God’s kingdom than go into a marriage that will ruin your relationship with God.

In 1 Corinthians 7:8–9, Paul admonishes single and divorced Christians to remain unmarried like him, but that they should get married if being single would cause them to fall into sexual sin.

This clearly tells us that marriage is a matter of choice, not something you have to do because you need to be complete.

You’re already loved by God. The idea of getting married is that you’re seeking someone who would share that love with you.

Getting married is a wise decision, but debunk that mindset that just one person out there is meant for you. So, when searching, you must focus on the person’s character and spiritual health, not if you feel a cosmic connection.

chapters.title: You need to actively search for a life partner
chapters.text:
Some Christians are so lazy they outsource to God the things they are supposed to do themselves. Later, when they get disappointed, they begin to resent and blame God unnecessarily.

It’s not logical to think you can sit in your comfort zone, and God will bring a life partner to you. It doesn’t work that way, yet you see a lot of Christians doing it.

There are few things in life that God does for us without our express participation, but finding a life partner isn’t one of them.

You need to put yourself in places and positions that make it easy for you to access potential life partners. We’ve settled the fact that there isn’t just one person out there that was uniquely created for you, so this should be easy. Your campus, workplace, and even churches that are filled with singles are good places to find potential life partners. The more you expose yourself, the more you’ll find options to choose from.

Note that the place you go to search is important. You aren’t likely to find a compatible man or woman in a raunchy bar, so go where you think your ideal match would be found.

According to The Knot Real Wedding Study, the average age for marriage in America as of 2020 is 33 for men and 31 for women. And other studies conducted within this period seem to agree with the age bracket. However, you know that we’re all sexual creatures, and our sexual drives set in a little over a decade before we get to those average ages, which is one reason why many people indulge in premarital sexual activities.

Some can keep chaste until they get married, while it’s a real battle for many others. But you know yourself better than anybody. If you’re sure you can’t keep yourself from sexual sins, then it’s better to get married early than to pretend to be someone you’re not.

The problem with pretension is that you can’t do it for long. Sooner or later, your real character would show forth — and these things can be messy. Early marriage can tame your sexual urges and keep you from jumping from one partner to another.

Financial difficulty is most people’s excuse for not marrying early. Sure enough, it won’t be easy financially for the average guy who marries in his early twenties, but if you’ve got a job and a dream, you can manage. The spiritual, emotional, and physical benefits of sticking to one sexual partner in a lifelong committed relationship are enormous. Don’t miss out on them.

chapters.title: Discuss marriage styles with your partner before tying the knot
chapters.text:
Many couples make the mistake of not discussing the marriage style they want before getting into it. Some are lucky to find that their ideal marriage style tallies with the one their partner wants, while others aren’t so lucky.

It’s risky to assume your partner wants the same thing as you simply because you two are “in love.” It’s always better to discuss and be 100% certain.

It’s possible that you don’t know the marriage style you want for yourself, but as you view the list below, you may find one or two that align with your innermost desires. Of course, this isn’t an exhaustive list of marriage styles, but they are the most common.
• Spiritual soulmate

Essentially, spiritual “soulmates” are couples who have decided to use their marriage to express the love of God in ways that the world can see it. This decision reflects in their lives as a couple and in their parenting style.
• Business buddies and romantic idealists

Business buddies describe the couple who comes together to build a business, a brand, a church, or a movement. The romantic idealists, on the other hand, are couples who derive their fulfillment from having an intimate relationship. For them, marriage is more important than anything else. These couples are the opposite of business buddies.
• Adonis and Aphrodite

Physical attraction is the primary focus of this couple. It’s the thing that brought them together in the first place, and it’s what would sustain the marriage. The problem with this couple, however, is that the human body changes over time, and when that happens, they are most likely to lose interest in one another.
• Cookbook couple

This couple lives by a set of very defined relationship rules. They are the “we can’t kiss on our third date,” “we must meet my parents by this time,” and “our friends can’t visit on so-so day” kind of couple. Usually, the people involved in this sort of union are actively reading advice books and visiting counselors every now and then.
• The horror show house

This is the kind of couple that enjoys terrorizing each other. Usually, one likes to do the terrorizing, while the other likes to be terrorized. They fight, argue, make each other miserable, then make up and continue the cycle again. This is a dangerous marriage style; you must never get involved in it.

Have you found a style you like yet?

Great. Now, if you’re in a relationship, your next task is to discuss with your partner to see what style appeals to them the most. It’s rare to have the same desire, but knowing your differences early can help you sort things out and prevent each other from getting hurt.

chapters.title: Look beyond the present when choosing a partner
chapters.text:
When you’re in love, it’s easy to only think about you and your love interest. But keep it at the back of your mind that if you eventually get married, kids will come — unless you decide not to have them. And not just that, you will face life challenges that may rattle the core of your being.

Like it or not, life will be hard. In fact, studies show that life is at least two times harder than most young people imagine it to be. Is your partner the kind of person who can endure difficult times, or are they a fair-weather friend?

God forbid, but if you’re bedridden for 24 months, can your partner sustain the physical, mental, and spiritual capacity to deal with the situation, or would they back off?

What if you have kids with special needs? Will your partner be able to handle it?

If you lost your job and are broke for a long time, would your partner cheat on you because they found someone who can provide the things you used to provide before you lost your job?

Don’t marry a fair-weather friend; your ideal spouse should be someone who can stick with you even in harsh conditions.

When you begin to think about these things, you realize that physical attraction is never enough to sustain a lifelong relationship. Don’t rush into marriage because your emotions are boiling. Take the time to objectively consider the character of the person you’re getting married to.

Lying about what you want out of marriage going in because you’re afraid you’ll lose the relationship if you are honest is one of the worst kinds of fraud you could ever commit. ~ Gary L. Thomas

Ask yourself if the person you’re committing yourself to is the best dad or mom you can find for your kids. Be sure to answer that objectively. Sometimes, it’s hard for us to be objective with our observations when we’re head over heels in love. This is why it makes sense to ask a family member or spiritual mentor you trust to give you their objective opinions about your partner.

chapters.title: Conclusion
chapters.text:
If you’ve taken the advice in this summary so far, then you’re ready for a successful marriage. Just one more thing! You need to understand that a successful marriage is built, not found. Infatuation can be found; sexual chemistry can be found, too. But it takes time, consistency, capability, and willingness to build the marriage of your dreams. A successful marriage is marked by intimacy and oneness; that's not something that happens overnight. It typically takes from 9 to 14 years of togetherness for two individuals to stop thinking of themselves as individuals and to start thinking of themselves as a couple. This is one of the reasons you must enter marriage with a lifelong commitment in view, not as something you want to do and get a divorce when things don't work as expected.

One person can’t do it alone; it takes you and your spouse to make a successful marriage happen. Some key character traits you need include:
• Humility
• Forgiveness
• Prayer
• Healthy conflict resolution skills
• Fidelity
• Friendship

You and your spouse must learn to cultivate these traits. And there is no rushing here. You'll both make mistakes in the early years; the primary reason is that your brain is still yet to adjust to the idea of being married. But the more consistent you are, the easier it becomes.

If you want to be free to serve Jesus, there’s no question—stay single. Marriage takes a lot of time. But if you want to become more like Jesus, I can’t imagine any better thing to do than to get married. Being married forces you to face some character issues you’d never have to face otherwise. ~ Gary L. Thomas

Try this

Before making the final decision to marry your love interest, check if they are at least average on:
• Spirituality
• Humility
• Forgiveness
• Conflict resolution

Try to be objective with your grading!

Read it till the end?
